findings.title: Pipelined URMA write failover path is untested
findings.impact: The new tracker recording after PipelineDataTransferHelper is only exercised when the payload exceeds the URMA buffer segment and uses the pipelined write path. The added ST uses a 512 KiB value, below the default 2 MiB urma_max_write_size_mb threshold, so it covers the single-write branch but would not fail if the pipelined branch stopped recording failures or successes.
findings.fix: Add a Set/Put failover test that forces the pipelined UB transfer path, for example with a value larger than the configured URMA max write size, injects a URMA write failure, and asserts the unhealthy window triggers URMA_DATA_PLANE_FAILURE switching.

findings.title: New URMA failover gflag validators lack reject-case tests
findings.impact: The PR adds operator-facing validation for the failover success-rate ratio and minimum sample count, including non-finite/out-of-range ratio values and zero sample count. The added tests only set valid values directly or through the client config, so invalid config could be silently accepted or the validator could be disconnected without any regression test failing.
findings.fix: Extend the flags tests or client-config hot-update tests to set invalid values such as -0.1, 1.1, NaN/inf for urma_failover_success_rate_ratio and 0 for urma_failover_min_sample_count, then assert SetCommandLineOption/config reload rejects them and preserves the previous valid value.
